name: What information do I need to gather before starting a zero-click visibility project?
description: Gather your top customer questions, accurate business details (NAP, hours, services, locations), current performance data from Search Console and Google Busines

mainEntity.name: What information do I need to gather before starting a zero-click visibility project?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The information you need includes a clear list of priority queries, accurate business data, and an inventory of existing content and profiles.
These inputs drive a focused, effective zero-click plan.

Context: Many strategies fail because they chase generic keywords without mapping real customer questions or checking how the business currently appears.
Good prep keeps efforts aligned with revenue.

Key factors:
- Top customer questions and high-intent search phrases
- Verified NAP, hours, services, and location details
- Current performance data (Search Console, GBP insights)
- List of all directories and profiles you control

Practical guidance: Before making changes, document this information in a single spreadsheet or workspace.
Use it to prioritize which questions and locations to optimize first and to track improvements over time.

Soft positioning: Think of this prep work as your blueprint for every zero-click surface where you want to be the default answer.
acceptedAnswer.description: Gather your top customer questions, accurate business details (NAP, hours, services, locations), current performance data from Search Console and Google Business Profile, and a list of all your existing profiles and directories. This becomes the blueprint for your zero-click visibility strategy.
